rationales.hedonism: She treats celebration and enjoyment as central, saying the rooftop bar "wasn't a detour from the work" but "the whole point of the work."
rationales.achievement: "Finished the coastal series today" after "four months of work," showing sustained effort to complete a major creative project.
rationales.security: She frames the work as worth "the missed deadlines" and even "the late rent notice," accepting instability for the sake of the art.
rationales.conformity: She rejects expected productivity by admitting she "should be preparing for the gallery pitch tomorrow" but choosing celebration instead.

rationales.hedonism: After the rejection she leans into immediate comfort, having "ordered delivery twice" and letting herself wallow instead of deferring pleasure to work.
rationales.achievement: She explicitly avoids the commission she has been "putting off" and admits "wallowing isn't productive," which directly conflicts with striving and performance.

rationales.self_direction: She refuses the in-house design path despite its appeal, saying "I'd rather figure out rent every month than wake up numb," prioritizing an authored life over external structure.
rationales.hedonism: She centers feeling alive and creative pleasure, describing sketching cranes and concluding "sitting here... I feel like myself."
rationales.security: She knowingly rejects "steady income, health insurance" and says she would rather keep struggling financially than return to stable office work.
rationales.conformity: She embraces being "the one who chose differently" and recalls quitting when "everyone told me I was making a mistake," showing willingness to defy expectations.

rationales.hedonism: She immediately "celebrated" the approval by buying an art set she had been wanting, foregrounding enjoyment in the moment.
rationales.achievement: "Commission client approved the sketches" marks successful performance on paid work and external validation of her competence.

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I'm not delaying life - I'm living it," defending her own chosen timeline instead of adopting her mother's expectations.
rationales.hedonism: She explicitly prefers "watching sunsets from my balcony" and spending hours on a canvas over a more conventional domestic path.
rationales.conformity: She resists the implied expectation about "my own timeline" and says she will not "build a life around a future version of myself" just to fit that script.

rationales.stimulation: She tries a new, initially resisted experience by going to a ceramics workshop and finds the novelty energizing rather than threatening.
rationales.hedonism: The day is framed around sensory and social enjoyment: being "covered in clay," "laughing at my lopsided bowl," and remembering that making art "is one of the best feelings in the world."

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the family firm's offer because "three years of building something of my own doesn't disappear," emphasizing autonomy over being slotted into a role.
rationales.security: She turns away from a presumably safer family position even while acknowledging it would be "perfect for someone with my skills."
rationales.conformity: Her refusal is also a refusal of family pressure, especially since her uncle "cc'd my father" to reinforce the expectation.

rationales.security: The entry centers material instability, from "the 2am math" to wondering if she can "make it to the 15th," showing her current path is undermining steadiness and protection.

rationales.hedonism: She responds to the gallery interest by planning a celebratory dinner at "the expensive one" and insists "this is worth celebrating."
rationales.achievement: A gallery reaching out because they "want to discuss a show" is explicit recognition that her work is "worth showing."
rationales.security: Even while admitting "it might fall through," she spends against the voice saying she "should save the money," privileging celebration over caution.

rationales.benevolence: When asked about the department head role, she says she is "focused on my students," prioritizing care for the people directly depending on her.

rationales.conformity: Although she "wanted to say" something sharp to Sofia's mother, she restrained herself, "just nodded," and kept the interaction smooth.
rationales.benevolence: She spends extra time "offering to meet with students individually" and keeps returning to the needs of Diego, who "actually needs the support."
rationales.universalism: She explicitly critiques inequity, noting "the system is set up for families who already have resources," which broadens her concern beyond one student.

rationales.benevolence: She calls Diego's mother after hours "because I wanted her to know he's been improving," extending care well beyond formal duties.
rationales.universalism: Her concern expands to structural change when she imagines redesigning the system so "students like Diego don't fall behind in the first place."

rationales.achievement: She marks a concrete performance milestone, noting it was the "first time he's gotten above a C on a written assignment."
rationales.benevolence: Her attention is on Diego's wellbeing, noticing that "he was proud" even though he "didn't make a big deal out of it."
She keeps returning to the concrete needs of "Diego" and the students who come because they "have nowhere else to go," valuing present care over career advancement.
rationales.universalism: Her case for the higher role is rooted in systemic fairness, pointing out that the curriculum "barely mentions indigenous perspectives" and assessments are "biased toward students with test prep resources."
